title: podman create/run need to load information from the image
body:
We should be pulling information out of the image to set the
defaults to use when setting up the container.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@mheon PTAL

The problem with this is it should be done before the users content is processed.  I think we need to reconsider the way we are creating the spec.
one comment, one question else lgtm

@rhatdan I think you're right - we can't do UID and GID lookups outside of libpod. Instead, I think we need to add libpod options to do this - WithUser and WithGroup, which can look it up inside the container when we do Init() and are creating the spec, around when we are calling your function to set things from the image. The container is mounted at that point, so we can chroot in and grab UID/GID info.

We're going to need to handle combining image settings and what the user provides in the spec. This probably involves a lot of checking - is environment var x already set in the container? That sort of thing.

Well if all of this was set before we processed the user data, we would not need to check since the user data would just replace the image data in the generate code.  The question I have is should we be handling the user data in libpod?  Will we have to handle the kubernetes overrides in libpod.

@rhatdan I think the biggest thing here might be that we need more knobs to decide what to include from the image. Maybe we have separate flags for including environment variables, command/entrypoint, etc - and we can let kpod, which knows what the user set and did not set, work with those flags.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #106) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably f820309) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@rhatdan Idea on this one - what if we have a function in libpod to get a default OCI spec from an image (IE, use a spec generator to make a default spec, and then put in all the image-specific defaults - command, environment, etc). We can use that to get the base spec in `podman create` and then apply user-specified settings over the ones from the image base spec. This doesn't require major changes to our API, just adding another function to help make the spec
@mheon yes I was working on something like that. I hope to get back to it when I am bored over the break.

@mheon @baude PTAL
I cleaned this up quite a bit, and I think it is closer to completion.  Exposed Ports still needs some work, but I with some tests added, we can move forward on the rest of image data.
@baude PTAL at the handling of exposed ports.
It looks OK from here/now ... I havent dove very deep in the port mapping yet in all honesty.

@mheon passes tests.
I think this is ready to merge.  We will need to continue to work on network ports.
Bunch of small stuff but overall LGTM

I suspect the port stuff is going to need to be looked at separately to make it work with what we have (might need to swap out the parse logic to make that work), but since ports aren't working anyway I don't view that as a big deal. LGTM

:pushpin: Commit 9871f3c has been approved by `mheon`
